THE RISE REPORT
YOUR WEEKLY READ ON THE REFEREE WORLD
Week of June 15, 2026
SUMMER SPOTLIGHT

Work the Rising Stars Cup — Washington, D.C.

Summer's here and Rise has big things in motion. First up: Rise Officials is proud to be the referee management partner for the Rising Stars Cup, August 6–9, 2026 in Washington, D.C. — 80 elite youth academies from across the U.S. and internationally, competing over three days of high-intensity action.

With a fall season right around the corner, this is the perfect preseason event to knock off the rust and get match sharp before the games that count.

WHY YOU'LL WANT TO BE THERE

⚫ Full referee uniform by Macron — provided to every official

⚫ On-site development — 3 dedicated referee coaches working with you throughout the event

⚫ Film review sessions — break down your matches and sharpen your game

⚫ Field sessions — hands-on training built into the weekend

⚫ Elite competition — top youth academy soccer, domestic and international

This isn't just an assignment — it's a chance to get better while working a premier event.

📅 Dates: Thursday, August 6 – Sunday, August 9, 2026

📍 Location: Washington, D.C.

A note on selection: we're only accepting 20–30 referees for this event, so spots are extremely limited and we expect strong interest. Get your application in early.

Apply Now →
Applications close Thursday, July 10, 2026 — don't wait.

📰 Around the World

A Record Eight Americans on the World Cup Roster

The summit of the sport is here — and it's on home soil. FIFA named a record eight US match officials to the 2026 World Cup. Two earned the center whistle: Ismail Elfath and Tori Penso, a historic pick as the first American woman named to a men's World Cup officiating roster. Four more Americans were selected to run the lines — assistant referees Kyle Atkins, Brooke Mayo, Kathryn Nesbitt, and Corey Parker.

The American presence reaches the video room, too. Joe Dickerson and Armando Villarreal serve as Video Match Officials (VAR), part of the crew supporting every call on the pitch. From the center circle to the monitors, the US is represented at every level of officiating at its own World Cup.

It was on full display on June 14 in Dallas, where an all-American crew took the field for Netherlands vs. Japan — referee Ismail Elfath with assistants Corey Parker and Kyle Atkins. For our referees, this is the very top of the ladder you're climbing. Every match you work and every position you learn builds the same foundation these officials once laid. The summit is real, it's American, and the path to it starts with the whistle in your hand this weekend.

FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands v Japan (June 14, Dallas). On-field crew: referee Ismail Elfath, ARs Corey Parker & Kyle Atkins — all USA.  Graphic: FIFA
🇺🇸 USA AT THE 2026 WORLD CUP

Referees: Ismail Elfath · Tori Penso

Assistant Referees: Kyle Atkins · Brooke Mayo · Kathryn Nesbitt · Corey Parker

Video Match Officials (VAR): Joe Dickerson · Armando Villarreal
